title: Daily Briefing — Mar 18
headline: Extreme Fear, weak breadth, and tech flirting with breakdowns—patience rules today.
body: Right, so we've got Fear & Greed parked at 25—Extreme Fear territory. The market's limping along: SPY at $670.79, well below the 20 MA ($680.19). VIX is elevated at 21.55 but contracting, which means volatility is cooling off but still far from calm. If it's in the press, it's in the price; the crowd is spooked, but smart money usually shops here, not panics.

Sector rotation is messy—Energy (XLE $58.51) is still leading but weakening, Utilities (XLU $47.13) and Staples (XLP $84.70) are rolling over, and Tech (XLK $139.54) is approaching the mean reversion zone, but bias is bear. Financials (XLF $49.56) remain elevated risk and haven't found support, which is the canary—keep a close eye there.

Contrarian signals stacking: Extreme Fear (rule R013) is historically an opportunity zone, but SPY, QQQ, and most mega-cap tech names are below their daily 20 MAs (R006), so longs are off until we see a proper reclaim. No chasing. Abundance mindset—let the flows come to you.
callout_body:
SPY: $680.19 (20 MA) — Daily close above needed for longs
XLK: $139.54 (20 MA) — Mean reversion zone, but bias bear
XLF: $49.56 — Canary, needs support
XLE: $58.51 — Still leading, but momentum fading
VIX: 21.55 — Elevated, but below 20 MA
Fear & Greed: 25 — Extreme Fear (contrarian opportunity, but wait for confirmation)
action_items: Watch for daily closes above 20 MAs on SPY, XLK, QQQ before considering new longs—no exceptions.
Monitor XLF for breakdown—if Financials lose support, reduce equity exposure immediately (R001).
closing_quote: Less trades = more wins. If bonds freak out, everything breaks—so don't force it.
